patterns: What shipping services does FedEx offer?
responses: FedEx provides a broad range of shipping services designed for domestic, international, express, freight, and e-commerce logistics operations.
Customers are responsible for ensuring that packages submitted to FedEx meet packaging, labeling, documentation, and service eligibility requirements.
Certain items are prohibited or restricted from transport due to safety, legal, customs, aviation, or regulatory requirements.
FedEx provides shipment tracking capabilities that allow customers to monitor package movement throughout the shipping lifecycle.
If a delivery cannot be completed, FedEx may attempt redelivery, hold the package, or return it to sender depending on service policies.
Customers may submit claims for lost, damaged, or delayed shipments subject to FedEx investigation and service agreement terms.
FedEx provides billing, invoicing, payment processing, and account charge management services for eligible customers.
Refund eligibility depends on service type, shipment circumstances, delivery performance, and FedEx service agreement policies.
FedEx offers pickup scheduling, drop-off locations, retail service points, and shipment acceptance options depending on region and service availability.
FedEx international shipping services may involve customs documentation, import/export regulations, duties, taxes, and destination country restrictions.
Certain shipments may require recipient signatures, identification checks, or delivery confirmation depending on service level and sender instructions.

patterns: How does FedEx use my data?
responses: FedEx may collect, process, store, and protect customer information in accordance with privacy laws, security requirements, and operational service needs.
Customers may use FedEx account services for shipment management, billing access, address storage, preferences, and online support functions.
Address correction or shipment rerouting may be available depending on shipment status, service type, destination rules, and applicable fees.
FedEx offers return shipping services and reverse logistics solutions that allow eligible shipments to be returned using approved return processes.
Shipment delays may occur due to weather conditions, customs inspections, operational disruptions, transportation issues, or regional service interruptions.
FedEx customer support services may assist with shipment tracking, claims, billing issues, delivery concerns, and account-related questions.
Customers must provide accurate names, delivery addresses, postal codes, contact details, and shipping instructions to avoid shipment delays or delivery failures.
FedEx uses operational handling procedures designed to reduce package damage, ensure transportation safety, and maintain shipment integrity.
FedEx digital platforms allow customers to create shipments, print labels, monitor tracking, manage addresses, review invoices, and access support services.
FedEx may use fraud detection systems, shipment screening processes, payment verification controls, and account monitoring tools to identify suspicious activities.
FedEx offers business shipping solutions including bulk shipments, scheduled pickups, freight coordination, warehousing integration, and enterprise logistics tools.
FedEx may implement sustainability initiatives focused on fuel efficiency, route optimization, emissions management, packaging reduction, and responsible logistics practices.
FedEx employees are expected to follow safety procedures, customer service standards, security rules, ethical conduct requirements, and operational compliance policies.
Driver activities may be monitored through GPS tracking, telematics systems, route management tools, and operational safety audits.
FedEx may investigate shipment disputes, damaged packages, delivery issues, and billing concerns subject to liability limits and service agreement policies.
By using FedEx services, customers agree to applicable shipping policies, payment rules, operational limitations, privacy practices, and legal service conditions.
